name: EarthDaily

overview.value: EarthDaily is a Canadian commercial satellite constellation of optical imaging Earth observation satellites, developed by EarthDaily Analytics (EDA), headquartered in Vancouver, based on assets acquired from the defunct company UrtheCast. Each of the 10 identical satellites carries an optical imaging instrument observing over 22 spectral bands (six visible, six near-infrared, six shortwave infrared and four thermal infrared) at 5 m spatial resolution. The constellation's first satellite launched on Falcon 9's Transporter-14 mission on 23 June 2025, and the next six launched on Falcon 9's CAS500-2 rideshare mission on 3 May 2026.

base.reason: eoPortal mission page (ESA-run aggregator); page states 'each optical imager will observe over 22 spectral bands with a spatial resolution of 5 m'; registry v2 EO spec backfill, cite-facts-only per terms
eoPortal mission page; specifications table states 'Swath Width ~240 km'
eoPortal mission page; page states the mission aims 'to provide images of the entire world on a daily basis' with 'Temporal Cadence Daily global coverage'
eoPortal mission page; specifications table states 'Spectral Bands 22 bands (Visible, NIR, SWIR, TIR)' (page prose also gives 21-22 bands)

notes: Added in the audit Phase 3 backfill (2026-07-08), verify-then-add. eoPortal EO spec backfill (2026-07-09): 10-satellite optical constellation, ~5 m resolution in key visible/NIR bands, ~240 km swath, 22 spectral bands (Visible, NIR, SWIR, TIR), daily global coverage. First satellite launched June 2025. Page last updated Jun 16, 2026.
